evals.prompt: Run a quick security scan on this project using semgrep (via podman container). I want to check for vulnerabilities with the owasp top ten rules and see results in the terminal.
evals.expected_output: Runs semgrep inside podman container with --config p/owasp-top-ten, colorized terminal output, uses -v mount for project directory
expectations: Uses podman run with docker.io/semgrep/semgrep:latest image
Output format is terminal/colorized (no --json or --sarif flag)
Reports findings in a structured format

evals.prompt: I have a custom semgrep rule file at .semgrep/my-rules.yaml. Please validate it and then scan the src/ directory with it using the semgrep podman container. I want JSON output saved to results.json for my CI pipeline.
evals.expected_output: First validates the rule file via container, then scans with JSON output saved to results.json
expectations: Validates the custom rule file: semgrep validate /src/.semgrep/my-rules.yaml (with /src/ prefix)
Writes output to /src/results.json (with /src/ prefix for output path)

evals.prompt: We're setting up CI security scanning with semgrep via podman. I need to scan only the changes since we branched from origin/main, using the default rule pack. Output SARIF format so we can upload to GitHub Advanced Security.
evals.expected_output: Incremental scan with --baseline-commit origin/main, --config p/default, SARIF output, using podman container with -w /src for git support
expectations: Uses podman with -w /src flag (required for git/baseline support)
Mentions SARIF can be uploaded to GitHub Advanced Security
